{{Quote|We suspect there might be some sort of Imperial Installation hidden in the asteroid field. Look for the shield projectors. Be careful, Wedge. The Imperials probably don't want any visitors.|General Rieekan to Rogue Squadron|<ref name="Leader" />}}
[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] was sent to patrol near [[Maw Cluster/Legends|The Maw]] for any signs of a rumored [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Maw Imperial Prison|prison]] facility and became involved in a '''battle''' there.

==Prelude==
In the aftermath of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was devastated, although the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] continued.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The Alliance leadership suspected that there was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Maw Imperial Prison|installation]] hidden in an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field<ref name="Leader" /> near [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]].<ref name="RS II guide" /> Three [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] members were sent into investigate, and as they approached the field, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] cautioned them to be attentive. [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], used his craft's [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] to disable a [[Mine/Legends|mine]] in their path.<ref name="Leader" />

==The battle==
===Space battle===
{{Quote|Attention Rebel pilot, surrender yourself, and we will show you leniency.|An Imperial, to Rogue Squadron|<ref name="Leader" />}}
In their [[BTL-A4 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]], [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and the other two [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] flew into the asteroid field. One of the pilots picked up a strange sensor reading, and Antilles cautioned them to stay alert, as the signals might have been Imperial. The Rogues then found a group of seven [[Imperial tanker]]s and a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], and their presence was revealed.<ref name="Leader" /> [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] approached to intercept the Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]<ref name="Leader" /> and an [[Unidentified Imperial (Kessel system)|Imperial]] demanded their surrender. The Imperials received no response from the Rebel pilots, who continued on towards their target: a [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] projected around the asteroid which housed the facility. Antilles disabled three of the [[Satellite force field shield projector|projectors]] and opened a hole in the shield, the and the Rogues descended towards the base on the surface of the asteroid, avoiding the mines spread around the facility.<ref name="Leader" />

===Engaging facility defense forces===
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader}}
Antilles reported their progress to Rieekan and then picked up a transmission from the surface. Rebel [[Karie Neth/Legends|Karie Neth]] and a number of other [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] captured after the Imperial assault on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] had managed to break out of the facility, but were desperate for support in order to escape safely. Neth sent out a message begging for someone to hear her. Neth identified herself to Antilles as the leader of the prisoners and asked Antilles and his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmen]] to destroy the [[Turbolaser/Legends|guard towers]] protecting the prison building. Neth transmitted targeting information to the Y-wings.<ref name="Leader" />

===Rebel prison escape===
[[File:Maw_Imperial_Prison_RS2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Maw prison]]
Antilles ordered his wingmen to attack while he destroyed the five towers protecting the prison so the [[armored hovertrain]] the escapees had commandeered could move in. A Rebel then reported they needed weapons, and transmitted more targeting information about the weapons storage building so Antilles could bomb the guard towers. Neth then told them to take out the comm relay before the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] patrol arrived. Antilles then flew to the other side of the base at the hangars and destroyed the five guns there so the prisoners could hijack an escape craft. He also [[Proton bomb/Legends|bombed]] Imperial [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|walkers]], adding to the general mayhem. Flying over the prison command buildings, he bombed them as well, and was able to recover [[Advanced cluster missile|technology]] useful to the Rebellion in an atmospheric dome. The surviving prisoners reached the landing platform and escaped in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]. Antilles engaged [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] attacking the shuttle until it could enter hyperspace.<ref name="Leader" />
{{Endgame}}

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|That's it, we're clear. Thanks for the help.|Neth to Antilles at the end of the battle|<ref name="Leader" />}}
Antilles reported to General Rieekan that their mission was accomplished, and he and his wingmen [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspaced]] out to the fleet in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. As the Rogues left, the facility was exploding behind them.<ref name="Leader" /> Karie Neth eventually joined Rogue Squadron,<ref name="DB-Neth, Karie">{{DB|character|karieneth|Neth, Karie}}</ref> and would eventually participate in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The battle near the Maw appears during a mission in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''. The mission is also included in the co-op campaign in ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]''. Picking up the upgrade will fulfill the mission's bonus objective. The player may order their wingman to attack targets, and this article assumes the player did so, rather than ordering them to retreat. During the race to the landing platforms, the [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide|strategy guide]] suggests following the train or proceeding directly. The player does not have to take out all guns and AT-PTs, although the strategy guide suggests doing so.

==Appearances==

*''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{C|Co-op campaign}}
